title: Sinch API credentials should not be disclosed
description: Sinch is a cloud communications platform for SMS, voice, video, and messaging APIs. Sinch credentials include Application Key/Secret pairs and Access Keys. Exposing these allows unauthorized SMS/voice messages at your expense and potential fraud.
recommendation: Remove Sinch credentials from source code. Store in environment variables. Rotate immediately at dashboard.sinch.com.
impact: Unauthorized SMS/voice calls at your expense, potential SMS phishing (smishing) campaigns, and access to communication logs.

checks.message: Sinch API secret detected. Sinch credentials authenticate SMS, voice call, and messaging API requests. Exposing the app secret allows unauthorized sending of SMS messages or initiating voice calls billed to your account, potential smishing (SMS phishing) campaigns from your numbers, and access to communication logs. Rotate immediately at dashboard.sinch.com.

Sinch Application Key detected. Sinch app keys identify your communication application. When combined with the App Secret (commonly found nearby), they form complete credentials for SMS and voice services. Ensure no app secret is hardcoded alongside this key.
